files.title: Overview
files.description: FlowMCP is a **Tool Catalog with pre-built API templates** and a **Knowledge Base for API workflows**. It unifies access to APIs through two equal channels:

files.title: Schema Format
files.description: This document defines the structure of a FlowMCP schema file, the two named exports (`main` and `handlers`), tool definitions, resource declarations, skill references, naming conventions, and...

files.title: Parameters
files.description: This document defines the parameter format for FlowMCP schema tools, resources, and skills. Each tool parameter describes where a value is placed in the API request (`position`) and how it is...

files.title: Shared Lists
files.description: Shared lists eliminate duplication of common value sets across schemas. Instead of every Etherscan schema maintaining its own chain list, they reference a single `evmChains` shared list. This...

files.title: Output Schema
files.description: Output schemas make tool responses predictable. AI clients can know in advance what shape the data will have, enabling structured reasoning without parsing guesswork. This document defines the output...

files.title: Security Model
files.description: FlowMCP enforces a layered security model that prevents schema files from accessing the network, filesystem, or process environment. All potentially dangerous operations are restricted to the trusted...

files.title: Agents
files.description: An Agent is a complete, purpose-driven definition that bundles tools from multiple providers for a specific task. Agents replace Groups from v2. Where Groups were simple tool lists, Agents are full...

files.title: MCP Tasks
files.description: **Deferred to v2.1.0.** This section is a placeholder.

files.title: Migration Guide
files.description: This guide covers migrating schemas between FlowMCP versions. Section 1 covers v2.0.0 to v3.0.0 migration. Section 2 preserves the v1.2.0 to v2.0.0 guide for reference.

files.title: Validation Rules
files.description: This document defines all validation rules enforced by `flowmcp validate`. Each rule has a code, severity, and description.

files.title: Tests
files.description: Tests are executable examples embedded in tool and resource query definitions. For agents, tests are prompts with expected tool usage and content assertions. They serve three purposes: they document...

files.title: Preload & Caching
files.description: This document defines the optional `preload` field on route level. It signals that a route returns a static or slow-changing dataset and that the runtime MAY cache the response locally.

files.title: Prompt Architecture
files.description: FlowMCP uses a two-tier prompt system to bridge deterministic tools with non-deterministic AI orchestration. **Provider-Prompts** explain how to use a single provider's tools effectively....

files.title: Resources
files.description: Resources provide local data access via SQLite databases and Markdown documents. They map to the MCP `server.resource` primitive and are defined in `main.resources` alongside `main.tools`. This...

files.title: Skills
files.description: Skills are reusable instructions for AI agents. They map to the MCP `server.prompt` primitive. Each skill is a `.mjs` file with a structured `export const skill` object that combines Markdown...

files.title: Catalog
files.description: A Catalog is the top-level organizational unit in FlowMCP v3. It is a named directory containing a `registry.json` manifest that describes all shared lists, provider schemas, and agent definitions...

files.title: ID Schema
files.description: A unified ID system for referencing all FlowMCP primitives. IDs MUST be unambiguous, human-readable, and resolvable. This document defines the ID format, component rules, Schema-File-ID, CLI-Adapter...

files.title: Scoring Protocol v1
files.description: Specification for grading FlowMCP v4 schemas via LLM evaluation. Documents the data formats exchanged between the CLI and an external Grader (e.g. Claude Code harness, third-party implementation).

files.title: License & Terms of Services
files.description: FlowMCP operates in a three-layer license model. **Schema authors and users MUST understand all three layers.**

files.title: Grading-Spec `gradingSpec/2.0.0`
files.description: This document uses the key words "MUST", "MUST NOT", "REQUIRED", "SHALL", "SHALL NOT", "SHOULD", "SHOULD NOT", "RECOMMENDED", "NOT RECOMMENDED", "MAY", and "OPTIONAL" as defined in BCP 14 [RFC2119]...

files.title: 01 — Default Journey & Maximalism
files.description: This chapter anchors the **default journey** by which a schema enters the FlowMCP corpus, the **maximalism principle** that governs its endpoint coverage, the link to the **interoperability** main...

files.title: 02 — Eligibility
files.description: This chapter defines **what is allowed to be part of a gradable schema**. Eligibility is the upstream gate: an endpoint that is not eligible MUST NOT appear in a schema. The maximalism principle in...

files.title: 03 — Terms of Service
files.description: This chapter defines the **Terms-of-Service (ToS) handling** of the Grading-Spec. ToS handling is part of the **due-diligence** layer (`Sorgfaltspflicht`), not part of the eligibility gate. A missing...

files.title: 04 — Provider-Side Grading Areas
files.description: This chapter is the **normative source for the provider-side grading Areas** — the Areas that grade one **schema** inside one **namespace** without group context. It replaces the linear phase model...

files.title: 05 — Selection-Side Grading Areas
files.description: A **selection** is a topic-oriented, curated collection of tools and skills assembled over several member namespaces. It is the **fifth primitive** introduced in the FlowMCP Schemas Specification...

files.title: 06 — Determinism and Tier
files.description: The Grading-Spec separates **reproducibility** (Determinism) from **attainability** (Tier). The two axes are **orthogonal**: a dimension can be deterministic but group-bound, or non-deterministic but...

files.title: 07 — Scoring System vs. Grading System
files.description: The terms **"Scoring"** and **"Grading"** are used **strictly separately** throughout this specification. They name two different sub-systems with two independent version namespaces. A scoring update...

files.title: 08 — Grading Model
files.description: A grading is an **array of evaluations** that carries **veto power**, a **tier trim** (autonomous max `B` / group max `A`), and can be **re-triggered by the user**. It is described as **one data...

files.title: 09 — Security and Development
files.description: Security and development discipline form an **independent grading area with high veto affinity**. Three of the four Categorical-Veto triggers defined in [`08-grading-model.md`](./08-grading-model.md)...

files.title: 10 — Domain Knowledge and Group Definition
files.description: A **topic group** (selection composed of several namespaces) develops conventions, shared vocabularies, and provider-specific quirks that are not visible from any single namespace in isolation....

files.title: 11 — About Convention as a Schema Resource
files.description: This chapter defines the **About Resource**: a markdown Resource that describes what a namespace (or a selection) does, what it does not do, and which conventions it follows.

files.title: 12 — Personas Contract
files.description: The Grading-Spec does NOT define personas of its own. It **references** the personas maintained in the sister repository `flowmcp-spec` at the path `repos/flowmcp-spec/personas/`. That folder is the...

files.title: 13 — Skills: Namespace Skill vs. Selection Skill
files.description: A skill carries a `type` (§2). The L1/L2/L3 **level** semantics described in this chapter apply **only to selection skills** (`type: 'selection'`). Namespace skills (`type: 'namespace'`) are a...

files.title: 14 — Kanban Data Contract (superseded)

files.title: 15 — Versioning + Canonical Hash (§10)
files.description: Versioning is carried by the **filename**, not by an in-source version key. Each primitive (schema, resource, skill, selection definition) is stored under the naming grammar defined in...

files.title: 18 — Flywheel Loop

files.description: The binding folder layout for grading data is the single source of truth for all other spec sections. The paths in `08-grading-model.md` (grading-entry files), `11-about-convention.md` §19...

files.description: This section is the **central anchoring point** for the pre-condition obligation. It was generalised from the Selection pre-condition to a **universal rule**: all aggregated checks...

files.title: 22 — Workbench Island
files.description: The grading data directory (`grading-data/`) is a **workbench island**: an internal working area where schemas and selections are hammered on day after day. It is deliberately separate from the...

files.title: 23 — The `index.json` Rollup
files.description: Status and grade live on the **namespace** level (and the **selection** level), not on a per-schema sidecar file. There is exactly **one `index.json` per namespace and one per selection**. It is the...

files.title: 24 — The `selection-aggregate` Area (11th area)
files.description: Grading is organised into **areas** — one rubric per primitive type. Ten areas already exist with output schemas under `prompts/output-schemas/`: `single-test`, `tools-aggregate-schema`,...

files.title: 25 — Harness and `/goal`
